page: Microsoft Azure D8nlds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance belonging to the Dnldv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This network-optimized, low-memory instance features 8 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The hardware configuration is designed for workloads requiring dedicated compute resources with a lower memory footprint relative to standard general-purpose instances.

For storage, the D8nlds_v6 is equipped with a 472 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. This local disk capacity provides fast scratch space for temporary files, while Premium Storage capability allows for high-performance external block storage attachments. The instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and it does not feature any GPU accelerators.

Given its network-optimized design, dedicated compute allocation, and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D8nlds_v6 is qualitatively cost-efficient for network-bound workloads, web serving, and distributed compute tasks that do not demand large memory capacities. It is well-suited for middle-tier application servers, network appliances, and lightweight database workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU cores and local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D8nlds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Dnldv6 family, featuring 8 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 16.0 GB of memory. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this network-optimized instance provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it suitable for compute-focused tasks with lower memory requirements. The server includes a 472 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage for high-performance external disk configurations. It does not feature GPU acceleration or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This instance is designed for network-bound applications, web serving, and lightweight database workloads that require dedicated CPU resources and fast local storage without the overhead of larger memory allocations. It offers a balanced, cost-effective profile for workloads that prioritize network throughput and dedicated processing power over high memory capacity.
